event: Icon of the Mother of God reading or holding a scroll
symbols: golden halo — sanctity and divine favor
dark blue-black robes (maphorion) — the traditional vestment of the Theotokos
scroll with text — the prophetic Scriptures concerning the Incarnation
red footstool — royal dignity and the earth as God's footstool
seated posture — contemplation and the indwelling of divine wisdom
description: An icon in an arched format showing the Virgin Mary (Theotokos) seated on a bench or throne, wearing the traditional dark blue-black maphorion over her garments. She has a golden halo and holds what appears to be a scroll or book with visible text. Her gaze is directed downward in contemplation. A stylized plant or flower is visible to the left, and decorative objects appear on the right. Her red shoes or boots are visible at the bottom. The style is characteristic of Ukrainian or Eastern European iconography with flat perspective and symbolic color.
theological_description: This icon of the Mother of God depicts Mary in a contemplative, scholarly posture — reading the prophetic Scriptures that foretold the very Incarnation she would bring about. Luke records that 'Mary treasured up all these things and pondered them in her heart' (Luke 2:19), and this icon captures that contemplative dimension of Marian theology. The scroll she holds may represent Isaiah's prophecy: 'Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign: The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and will call him Immanuel' (Isaiah 7:14). In Orthodox theology, Mary as Theotokos (God-Bearer) is the living fulfillment of the Old Testament's longing — she is the Ark of the New Covenant who carries within herself the very presence of God. Her seated, reading posture also connects her to the Wisdom tradition: she who was chosen to bear Divine Wisdom in the flesh is herself the attentive student of God's word, embodying the blessed one 'whose delight is in the law of the LORD' (Psalm 1:2).
